description_long: This object is a traditional copper alembic kettle, featuring a large, rounded main chamber with a smooth, polished metallic surface. A prominent, arched spout extends from the top of the chamber, curving gracefully and connecting to a cylindrical condenser vessel with two horizontal support rods. The main chamber has a small, circular gauge or dial on its upper section, likely for monitoring temperature or pressure. The lid is domed and seamlessly integrated, with a small knob at the top, suggesting it is removable. The entire object has a uniform, shiny copper finish with no visible decorative patterns, giving it a classic and utilitarian appearance.
description: A polished copper alembic kettle with a rounded chamber, arched spout, and cylindrical condenser, featuring a domed removable lid and a smooth, shiny finish.
description_view_0: The rounded main chamber with a domed lid, arched spout, and attached cylindrical condenser are clearly visible, along with a small gauge on the upper section.
description_view_1: The view highlights the alignment of the arched spout and the cylindrical condenser, showing the side profile of the kettle and the condenser's support rods.
description_view_2: The arched spout and cylindrical condenser are prominent, with the domed lid and main chamber visible from a slightly different angle, emphasizing the smooth copper finish.
description_view_3: The back of the main chamber is visible, with the arched spout extending forward and the cylindrical condenser mostly hidden behind the main body.
